blocks.text: A trusted runtime envelope is a provider-specific process shape that has explicit authority and conformance tests.

items: Codex currently adopts the full-access app-server envelope owned by daemon-generated arguments.
Claude requires explicit PermissionMode and rejects unsafe bypass unless policy allows the isolated trust tier.
Cursor and OpenClaw remain unpromoted until equivalent command, permission, and integration evidence exists.

blocks.text: Promotion requires security review, command-shape tests, and provider adapter conformance evidence.
